Description: Test individual classes and methods in isolation using PHPUnit or Pest
Setup: composer require --dev phpunit/phpunit\n# phpunit.xml configured with testsuites
Common Mistake: Testing implementation details instead of behavior; tight coupling to framework internals

Description: Write tests using Pest's expressive and minimal syntax for better readability
Setup: composer require --dev pestphp/pest\n./vendor/bin/pest --init
Common Mistake: Mixing PHPUnit and Pest syntax in the same file; not using expectations

Description: Test complete HTTP request/response cycles through the application
Setup: Uses RefreshDatabase trait; seeds test data with factories
Common Mistake: Not using RefreshDatabase causing test pollution; not testing response structure

Description: Create test doubles to isolate the system under test from its dependencies
Setup: composer require --dev mockery/mockery
Common Mistake: Mocking too many things making tests brittle; not calling Mockery::close()

Description: Run the same test with multiple inputs and expected outputs using data providers
Setup: Define a data provider method returning arrays or use Pest datasets
Common Mistake: Duplicating test code for each input instead of using data providers

Description: Test database interactions with proper isolation using transactions and factories
Setup: Use RefreshDatabase or DatabaseTransactions trait
Common Mistake: Using a real production database for tests; not using transactions for isolation

Description: Use model factories to generate realistic test data with customizable states
Setup: Define factories with Faker for each model
Common Mistake: Creating test data manually: $user = new User(); $user->name = 'Test'; ...

Description: Mock external HTTP calls to avoid hitting real APIs in tests
Setup: Use Laravel Http::fake() or Guzzle mock handlers
Common Mistake: Making real HTTP calls to external APIs in tests (flaky and slow)

Description: Test that jobs are dispatched without actually processing them
Setup: Use Queue::fake() to intercept dispatches
Common Mistake: Actually running queued jobs in feature tests (slow, side effects)

Description: Test that events are dispatched without triggering their listeners
Setup: Use Event::fake() to stop listeners from running
Common Mistake: Testing side effects (emails sent, cache cleared) when you only want to verify dispatch

Description: Test that notifications are sent without actually delivering them
Setup: Use Notification::fake()
Common Mistake: Sending real emails/SMS in test environment

Description: Test authenticated endpoints by simulating logged-in users
Setup: Use actingAs() or Sanctum::actingAs()
Common Mistake: Manually creating tokens or logging in via HTTP for every test

Description: Understand when to use mocks stubs spies and fakes for different testing needs
Setup: Choose the right test double for your scenario
Common Mistake: Using mocks for everything even when a simple stub or fake would be clearer

Description: Measure test coverage to identify untested code paths
Setup: composer require --dev phpunit/phpunit\nEnable pcov or xdebug\nphpunit.xml: <coverage><include><directory>app</directory></include></coverage>
Code Example:
# Run with coverage:
XDEBUG_MODE=coverage ./vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-html reports/

# Or with pcov (faster):
php -dpcov.enabled=1 ./vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-text

# Set minimum threshold:
./vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-text --coverage-min=80
Common Mistake: Aiming for 100% coverage testing getters/setters; ignoring integration test coverage

Description: Test full user workflows through a real browser with Laravel Dusk
Setup: composer require --dev laravel/dusk\nphp artisan dusk:install
Common Mistake: Running Dusk tests in CI without headless Chrome; slow tests due to unnecessary browser tests

Description: Organize tests into logical groups for fast feedback and clear structure
Setup: Separate Unit and Feature directories. Use naming conventions. Group with #[Group]
Common Mistake: Putting all tests in one directory; mixing unit and feature tests; no clear naming

Description: Assert API response structure status codes and data content
Setup: Use assertJson assertJsonStructure assertJsonPath
Common Mistake: Only checking status codes without verifying response body structure

Description: Use setUp and tearDown for test fixture management and cleanup
Setup: setUp() runs before each test. setUpBeforeClass() runs once per class
Common Mistake: Duplicating setup code in every test method; forgetting to call parent::setUp()

Description: Compare output against a stored snapshot to detect unexpected changes
Setup: composer require --dev spatie/pest-plugin-snapshots or spatie/phpunit-snapshot-assertions
Common Mistake: Snapshot tests that include timestamps or random data causing false failures

Description: Run tests in parallel across multiple processes to reduce total test time
Setup: composer require --dev brianium/paratest\nOr use Laravel's --parallel flag
Common Mistake: Tests that depend on shared state (same database records, same cache keys) break in parallel
